name: Suspicious Parent for a Process
notes:
This rule triggers when a process that is not supposed to have child launches a process. 
Both lsass.exe and lsm.exe are known to rarely spawn child processes. Any spawn of a command shell, a PowerShell instance, or a script execution might be worth triggering an investigation. 
These particular processes are monitored because they both execute in the security context of the local SYSTEM account, giving potential child processes privileged accesses to the system.
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
when the event matches LOWER("Parent Process Name") IMATCHES '((lsm)|(lsass))\.exe' AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY Suspicious Parent for a Process on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
AND when the event matches LOWER("Parent Process Name") IMATCHES '((lsm)|(lsass))\.exe' AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: Suspicious Parent for a Process
Event description: Detects when a process that is not supposed to have child launches a process.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
